node_2: lightweight end-to-end active speaker detection approach
edge: This section describes the proposed lightweight end-to-end active speaker detection approach in detail.

node_2: feature representation frontend and speaker detection backend
edge: The proposed framework consists of a feature representation frontend and a speaker detection backend.
The 3D convolution is split into 2D and 1D convolutions for extracting spatial and temporal information.
The method involving 3D convolution splitting can reduce the number of model parameters and computational burden while maintaining good performance.

node_2: visual blocks for spatiotemporal feature extraction
edge: The encoder comprises three visual blocks with two paths for spatiotemporal feature extraction.
The convolution splitting is done with a kernel size of 3 to extract features with different receptive fields.
The convolution combination happens after 3D convolution splitting with a kernel size of 5, creating multiple paths to extract features.
Multiple paths are designed to extract features with different receptive fields to obtain abundant spatiotemporal information.
Features from different paths are integrated by convolution with a kernel size of 1.
Features are integrated by convolution with a kernel size of 1 to obtain spatiotemporal information.
Batch normalization and ReLU activation are performed for each convolution in the visual block.
Convolution operations including batch normalization and ReLU activation are done in the visual block.
All convolutions in the visual feature encoder have a stride of 1, except for the 2D convolution in the first visual block.
The 2D convolution in the first visual block has a stride of 2, reducing spatial dimensions during feature extraction.
Smaller feature maps are generated by the visual feature encoder due to the reduction in spatial dimensions when extracting features.
The small-size feature map reduces the memory footprint and computation speed.
Reducing the memory footprint through small-size feature maps improves the computation speed.
